core_mechanism: a probability-generating-function formalism for a branching process on a random network, whose self-consistency (fixed-point) equation locates a percolation phase transition at a critical threshold separating a phase with no macroscopic cluster from one in which a giant connected component of finite fractional size emerges
brief_reason: The abstract builds the theory of random graphs from an arbitrary degree distribution and derives exact expressions for the position of the phase transition at which a giant component first forms, along with mean component size and giant-component size, which is precisely the generating-function/branching analysis of a percolation threshold.
